meta.reportLabel: Thu post-NFP update — June payrolls +57k MISS (vs +115k) but unemployment FELL to 4.2%: a mixed print. Hike bets pared (Sept ~50/50, 2-yr down); Warsh's hawkish hold intact - the dollar dips, doesn't break. EUR/USD short trimmed to MEDIUM; AUD/USD to RANGE-tilt; DXY to LOW. Oil glut deepens: Brent ~$70.6 / WTI ~$67.6 at pre-war levels (Hormuz flows >10mn b/d) - CAD still WEAKEST. EZ June flash cooled to 2.8% (from 3.2%). Yen at a 40-yr low; Fri's thin US-holiday tape = prime intervention window. RBNZ Jul 9 leans HIKE (~70%).
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40
meta.regime: Post-NFP: a dented-but-standing dollar inside an oil-glut disinflation regime. June payrolls missed at +57k (vs +115k) but unemployment FELL to 4.2% and wages held (+0.2% m/m) - a mixed print that pared hike bets (Sept ~50/50, 2-yr yield down, equities at records) without touching Warsh's hawkish hold (headline CPI still ~4.2%). Oil is the deeper force: Brent ~$70.6 / WTI ~$67.6, a third straight down-day to pre-war levels on genuine oversupply (Iran 40mn+ bbl post-blockade, Hormuz flows >10mn b/d, record Russian exports) - pinning CAD as the weakest (USD/CAD ~1.42, near a 14-mo low). The yen grinds at a 40-yr low (USD/JPY ~162.5, touched 161.95); Tokyo eyes Friday's thin US-holiday tape as the prime intervention window. EZ June flash cooled sharply to 2.8% (EUR soft, ~1.14). RBNZ Jul 9 leans hike (~70%).
meta.nextBigEvent: Fri Jul 3 US-holiday thin tape (prime MoF yen-intervention window), then RBNZ Jul 9 (lean hike ~70%) - the next scheduled swing

dailyRead: The NFP fade played out: June payrolls printed a soft +57k (vs +115k consensus) - but the report was MIXED, with unemployment FALLING to 4.2% (vs 4.3% expected) and wages steady (+0.2% m/m, ~3.5% y/y). The 2-yr yield fell and hike bets were pared (Sept ~50/50), yet the drop in the jobless rate keeps Warsh's hawkish hold fully intact - the dollar dips, it doesn't break. The other force is unchanged and deepening: oil is in a genuine supply glut - Brent ~$70.6 / WTI ~$67.6, a third straight down-day to pre-war levels (Iran 40mn+ bbl shipped post-blockade, Hormuz flows >10mn b/d, record Russian exports) - which pins CAD as the clear weakest (USD/CAD ~1.42, near a 14-mo low). The yen keeps grinding at a 40-yr low (USD/JPY ~162.5, touched 161.95), with Friday's thin US-holiday tape still the prime intervention window. EUR is soft (EZ June flash HICP cooled sharply to 2.8% from 3.2%). Best-expressed: USD/CAD long, AUD/CAD long, NZD/CAD long (the oil leg carries them, not the USD leg), EUR/USD short (conviction trimmed post-NFP); the long-JPY core stays reduce/defend, do NOT add - the Friday window is the re-load. Next swing: RBNZ Jul 9 (lean hike, ~70%).

strength.tag: mixed NFP: +57k miss, unemp fell to 4.2%
strength.drivers: June NFP +57k (big miss vs +115k) BUT unemployment FELL to 4.2%; wages +0.2% m/m (~3.5% y/y). Core PCE 3.4% (May); headline CPI ~4.2%. Fed 3.50-3.75%, hawkish hold, dots flip next move to a HIKE (median 3.8%).
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Mixed NFP keeps the hawkish HOLD intact: the payrolls miss pared the hike tail (Sept ~50/50, 2-yr yield down) but a falling jobless rate + 4%+ CPI bar cuts entirely - the dollar dips, doesn't break. Risk: a SECOND soft labor print kills the hike tail and unwinds USD longs. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.drivers: Core ~2.5% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); CPI 2.8% (held). BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (Greene + Pill hike votes); next BoE Jul 30 + MPR.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - services re-accelerated to 3.7%, reopening a hike-tail and keeping 2 dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR. Risk: a soft activity wave re-splits it. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: sticky core, more hikes priced, capped by USD
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%, 60bp above target top); headline ~4.0%; unemp 4.5%. RBA 4.35% (hiked May, 8-1); market prices the cash rate to 4.70% by year-end.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Sticky 3.6% trim + market pricing 4.70% by Dec keeps an Aug-hike tail open; AUD/USD ~0.692, capped sub-0.70 by the dollar. Risk: cool Q2 jobs / risk-off / the oil glut hits commodity beta. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: Headline ~2.5%; RBNZ saw a 4.3% Q3 peak (pre oil-drop); unemp ~5.1% elevated. RBNZ 2.25% (held May 27, casting-vote split). Still 'widely expected' to hike 25bp Jul 9, but odds pared to ~70% from 80%+ by the oil slide.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Jul 9 leans HIKE but odds cut to ~70%: still widely expected to hike 25bp (credibility, half the MPC wanted to move in May), but oil's slide to the high-$60s (vs the $101 May assumption) is the swing against; a weak TWI-low kiwi adds imported-inflation pressure for a hike. Live two-way, lean hawkish. Next RBNZ Jul 9.

strength.drivers: Core ~1.5%; CPI ~2.8%; Tokyo core re-accelerating; Tankan surged. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16); but USD/JPY ~162.7 (touched 161.95, lowest since 1986) on the ~250-275bp rate gap + fiscal-dovish Japan.
strength.forward: 🦅↔ Destination up, spot at a 40-yr low. Tokyo core re-accelerating backs more BoJ hikes, but the rate gap + fiscal-dovish Japan dominate. Friday's thin US-holiday liquidity = prime intervention window. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down sharply from 3.2%, below 3.0% exp): energy 8.7% (from 10.8%), services 3.2% (from 3.5%). Core ~2.2% (May); unemp ~6.3%. ECB 2.25% (hiked Jun 11), 'gradual' frame.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The 2.8% flash guts the next-hike case - EUR soft into a Jul 23 hold. EUR/USD ~1.14 support; Lagarde's Sintra frame keeps optionality but the data leans dovish. Risk: services re-accelerate / hawkish Jul 23 tone. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: hot CPI overwhelmed by an oil glut
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven); trimmed core 2.0%, median 2.1%; May jobs +88k strong, unemp down to 6.6%, but wages cooled to 3.0%; technical recession. BoC 2.25% (4th hold, Jun 10), looking through energy. USMCA July review a tail.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Hot CPI floor overwhelmed by the oil GLUT: Brent ~$70.6 (WTI ~$67.6) at pre-war levels pins the loonie near a 14-mo low (USD/CAD ~1.42); BoC looks through energy. Risk: an oil re-spike rescues it. Next BoC Jul 15.

symbols.why: Still the cleanest pair - the CAD leg now carries it: the +57k NFP miss dented the USD leg, but the oil GLUT (Brent ~$70.6, pre-war levels) pins CAD near a 14-mo low. Hot CPI 3.2% only a floor (core ~2%); technical recession. ~1.42.
symbols.risk: An oil re-spike (Hormuz flare / Lebanon) rescues CAD; a SECOND soft US labor print kills the USD leg.

symbols.why: Cleanest commodity split: sticky AUD trim 3.6% + a live RBA tail (4.70% by Dec) vs oil-glut-pressed, look-through-CPI CAD; Brent sub-$72 widens it toward parity.
symbols.risk: Risk-off + the oil glut also hit AUD beta; a CAD-supportive oil bid.

symbols.why: Upgraded: CAD pinned by the Brent ~$71 glut AND the NZD leg now leans hawkish (Jul 9 hike ~70%) - both legs finally align rather than the oil-leg carrying it alone.
symbols.risk: A dovish oil-driven RBNZ hold undercuts NZD; an oil bid lifts CAD.

symbols.why: Held, conviction trimmed post-NFP: the EZ flash cooled sharply to 2.8% (EUR leg softer) while the +57k miss only dents the USD leg (unemp fell to 4.2%). Divergence intact; ~1.14 pivot.
symbols.risk: The soft-NFP bounce extends (Sept ~50/50); a hawkish ECB re-pricing into Jul 23.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination + a softer EUR - but CHALLENGED: the yen's break to a 40-yr low is running every XXX/JPY higher. Reduce/defend.
symbols.risk: Yen-weakness momentum overwhelms the short until the Friday intervention window; a hawkish EUR surprise.

symbols.why: Structural core (BoJ hiking, coord watch) but RUN OVER: ~162.7, touched 161.95 (lowest since 1986). Reduce/defend, do NOT add; Fri's thin tape is the intervention window / re-load.
symbols.risk: Momentum toward 163-164; a JOINT intervention spike is the only violent snap-back.

symbols.why: Structural-JPY destination vs a CAD pinned by the sub-$72 oil glut - but the yen 40-yr-low grind challenges it near-term. Low conviction.
symbols.risk: Yen weakness runs it up; an intervention spike snaps it lower (the re-load).

symbols.why: Downgraded SHORT->RANGE post-NFP: the +57k miss softened the USD headwind while the 3.6% trim + 4.70%-priced RBA arm a floor - trade levels sub-0.70.
symbols.risk: Risk-on + a second soft US print flips it LONG; the oil glut hitting AUD beta argues lower.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: services CPI 3.7% re-armed a hawkish BoE vs a dollar dented by the +57k miss - no clean edge; post-NFP bounce favors the range top.
symbols.risk: A hawkish BoE Jul 30 breaks it up; soft UK activity re-splits it.

symbols.why: Jul 9 RBNZ leaning hike (~70%) + a soft-NFP dollar = mild upward tilt, but a still-hawkish Fed caps it - level-trade into RBNZ.
symbols.risk: A dovish oil-driven RBNZ hold presses it; a USD re-firm on hot US CPI.

symbols.why: Services-CPI re-acceleration firmed the GBP leg; the yen 40-yr-low grind tilts it higher near-term - two-sided.
symbols.risk: Firm GBP + weak yen spot tilts it up; a coordinated intervention spike snaps it lower.

symbols.why: Weak-yen momentum leans it up near-term, capping the short; only risk-off carry unwind + RBA floor argue lower.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry + yen weakness holds it up; a coordinated spike is the abrupt re-load.

symbols.why: Structural JPY destination vs a firmer NZD (Jul 9 hike lean) - carry + yen weakness offset near-term, low conviction.
symbols.risk: Risk-on carry dominates near-term -> stalls; an intervention spike re-loads.

symbols.why: ECB gradual + cooling flash vs a hawkish-hold RBA (sticky trim, 4.70% priced) - mild AUD tilt, no USD vector.
symbols.risk: EZ vs Aussie data set direction; the oil glut hitting AUD beta.

symbols.why: ECB spent (cooling flash) vs an NZD now leaning hawkish (Jul 9 hike ~70%) - mild NZD tilt building.
symbols.risk: NZD risk beta or a Jul 23 ECB shift; an oil-driven RBNZ hold.

symbols.why: Two firm legs: re-armed BoE (services 3.7%) vs hawkish-hold RBA (3.6% trim) - no clean edge.
symbols.risk: UK services vs Aussie sticky-core split sets direction.

symbols.why: Hawkish-re-armed GBP vs an NZD leaning hawkish (Jul 9) - both firm, no clean edge now.
symbols.risk: Trade levels only; the Jul 9 RBNZ outcome sets it.

symbols.why: Both commodity-bloc, but RBNZ Jul 9 (~70% hike) leads the RBA (Aug 11 tail) on timing - a slight NZD tilt.
symbols.risk: Aussie data vs the Jul 9 RBNZ; an oil-driven RBNZ hold flips it.

symbols.why: Downgraded post-NFP: the +57k miss knocked yields and pared Sept to ~50/50 - the long is carried only by the falling 4.2% jobless rate + weak counterparts (EUR soft, JPY 40-yr low, CAD oil-pinned).
symbols.risk: A second soft labor print unwinds a stretched long; positioning extreme.

symbols.why: Being run over: basket at fresh lows as the yen sits at a 40-yr low (touched 161.95) - core only, reduce/defend, do NOT add. Fri's thin tape is the intervention window. (Rises when JPY strengthens.)
symbols.risk: Timing wrong until a coordinated intervention or a USD pullback; thesis intact.

symbols.why: Oil-glut tailwind now stronger (Brent ~$71, war premium fully crushed) + EZ downturn easing + a cooling inflation flash.
symbols.risk: A Hormuz-talks breakdown / Lebanon escalation re-spikes oil; a hawkish-Warsh rates scare.

catalysts.note: Mixed print: payrolls miss pared hike bets (Sept ~50/50, 2-yr down) but the falling jobless rate keeps the hawkish hold intact; equities at records.
Tokyo's prime intervention window: a thin tape amplifies any MoF yen-buying.
Brent ~$71 / WTI ~$68 on Iran (40mn+ bbl shipped post-blockade) + record Russian exports + floating storage - structurally bearish, the CAD pain.
Live two-way, lean hike (~70%): still widely expected to hike 25bp, but the oil slide pared odds from 80%+ - the NZD swing.
Looking through energy CPI; technical recession + the oil glut cap it.
'Gradual' frame (hold); cooling German/EZ flash keeps the EUR soft into it; Sept ~50%.
Warsh's cycle; hot PCE + hike-dot vs a NOW-CONFIRMED softening labor tape (NFP +57k) - Sept ~50/50.
Services 3.7% keeps 2 hawk dissents live - the GBP swing.
Tokyo core re-accelerating; the yen's structural destination vs the 40-yr-low spot.
USD/JPY ~162.7 (touched 161.95, lowest since 1986); Friday's thin tape is the prime window - biggest near-term JPY-book swing.
60-day 'best efforts' toll-free passage (Iran still charging 'service fees'); 34 crossings Jun 30; Doha talks ongoing - a pause, not peace, but oil now falls on glut not war.

geopolitics: The story has flipped from war premium to supply glut. The June 17 Trump-Pezeshkian MOU has Iran making 'best efforts' for toll-free Hormuz passage for 60 days (Tehran still charging 'service fees'); ~34 verified crossings by Jun 30 show operational continuity, and indirect US-Iran talks continue in Doha (Qatar/Pakistan mediating) - a pause, not a peace. But the price action is now driven by oversupply, not geopolitics: Iran has shipped 40mn+ barrels since the US lifted its naval blockade, Russian exports are at records, and barrels are accumulating in floating storage. Brent has cracked to ~$71 (WTI ~$68), sliding 2-3% on the session, with the war premium fully crushed and gold's residual bid (~$4,200) the only remaining thin hedge. Transmission: oil -> energy/headline inflation -> CB hawkishness -> FX. Base case (now dominant): the glut deepens, oil drifts toward the high-$60s, risk stays two-way; keeps a firm dollar bid, supports GER40 and risk-beta AUD on calm days, and does NOT buy a Fed cut (hot May PCE confirmed inflation marked up into the oil drop; disinflation is energy-led). For NZD the lower oil path removed the cost-shock case the RBNZ leaned on (its 4.3% Q3 peak now stale) - hike odds pared to ~70%, though a hike is still the base case and a weak TWI-low kiwi adds imported-inflation pressure for moving. For CAD the broadening CPI (3.2%) is now overwhelmed by the glut -> weakest currency, the export side pressed hardest at ~$71 (loonie near a 14-mo low); the July USMCA review is an added tail. Tail (contained but alive): Doha talks stall / Lebanon escalates / a fresh Hormuz incident re-spikes oil (rescues CAD, hurts AUD/NZD/GER40, hands USD & JPY competing haven bids) - but with floating storage high, even a spike may fade faster than in prior episodes; the residual ~$4,200 gold bid prices the thin premium.
